cialdini.com
Principles of Persuasion (POP) Message Map 6.0
Relationships
Reciprocity: People feel obligated to give back
to you when you have given to hem first.
Principles of Persuasion
are decision triggers
for “yes”.
It’s what you do
first!
§ 	Contrast is always available
§ 	If you can’t think of a contrast,
you’re not thinking hard enough
§ 	“Compared to what?”
They help us make efficient
and good decisions.
Consistency: Individuals want to look consistent. Once a
person takes a stand, they behave in ways true to that
position.
Action
Social Proof: People look to what others are doing to decide
what they should do – especially when they are uncertain
and when others are ‘like them’.
Uncertainty
ACTivator: 	Evidence of how others think, feel, act in relevant
settings (polls, surveys, stats, testimonials).
AMPlifiers: 	Many others, Similar others, Uncertainty.
ACTivator: Commitment
AMPlifiers: - Active: People live up to what they write down
- Public: Accountability
- Voluntary: “He who complies against his will is
of the same opinion still”
ACTivator: 	Gifts 	Concessions
AMPlifiers:
a) Meaningful
b) Unexpected
c) Customized
“Moments of Power”
Scarcity: Things appear more valuable
when they are less available.
Action
Authority: People look to credible experts
and unbiased sources of information to decide
what they should do.
Uncertainty
ACTivator: 	Cues: Clothes, Titles, Accomplishments
AMPlifiers: 	Credibility: Expertise, Trustworthiness
Mention weakness early on
ACTivator: Rare/dwindling availability
AMPlifiers: Loss framing
Competition
Exclusive Information (Double Whammy!)
Unity: People are inclined to say yes to someone they
consider one of them.
ACTivator: 	Localism
Acting together
Co-creation
AMPlifiers: 	Intensity of Relationship
Frequency of Contact
Ask for Advice
Relationships
ACTivator: 	Similarity
Praise
Cooperation
AMPlifiers: 	Like them first!
Relationships
Liking: Others say yes to us more when
they know we genuinely like them.
www.getwsodo.com
